paper.title: IRS 9: The Case for a Dynamically-Ejected Star from the Galactic Center

extraction.summary: The paper presents proper-motion measurements for 23 SiO masers near SgrA* and finds that only IRS 9 is locally unbound from the GC at its projected radius. However, orbit integrations show IRS 9 is bound to the GC at larger radii (r_peri >= 0.10 pc, r_apo >= 5.25 pc). The paper does not present any object as a candidate to escape the Milky Way potential. The other 22 masers, including previously proposed high-velocity sources SiO-16, SiO-21, and SiO-25, have updated 3D velocities below their local escape velocities.

candidate_groups_considered.description: 23 SiO masers near SgrA* with HST proper motions and updated 3D velocities, including four previously reported as locally unbound (IRS 9, SiO-16, SiO-21, SiO-25).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper finds only IRS 9 is locally unbound from the GC at its projected radius (v_3d = 370.4 +/- 1.2 km/s vs v_esc,max = 331.1 +/- 6.5 km/s), but orbit integrations show IRS 9 is bound to the GC at larger radii with r_peri >= 0.100 +/- 0.005 pc and r_apo >= 5.25 +/- 0.18 pc. The paper does not present any of the masers, including IRS 9, as candidates to escape the Milky Way potential. The remaining 22 masers have v_3d < v_esc,max, including the previously proposed high-velocity sources SiO-16, SiO-21, and SiO-25.

source_refs.context: Only IRS 9 has v_3d significantly above v_esc,max; the remaining masers are below their escape velocities
IRS 9 is locally unbound but not necessarily destined to escape the GC; it is bound at larger radii
Conclusions: only IRS 9 exhibits v_3d > v_esc,max, indicating it is locally unbound; none of the other masers meet the criteria
